text: Workers' Compensation and Employer's Liability, $1,000,000 Per Accident = Statutory Limits $1,000,000 Each Accident and Disease USL&H must be provided where such exposure exists.. Workers' Compensation and Employer's Liability, Current ISO edition of CA 00 01 Arising out of any auto (Symbol 1), including owned, hired and nonowned = State where work is to be performed must be listed under Item 3.A. on the Information Page. Such insurance shall cover liability arising out of the Subcontractor's employment of workers and anyone for whom the Subcontractor may be liable for workers' compensation claims. Workers' compensation insurance is required, and no 'alternative' forms of insurance shall be permitted. Where a Professional Employer Organization (PEO) or 'leased employees' are utilized, Subcontractor shall require its leasing company to provide Workers' Compensation insurance for said workers and such policy shall be endorsed to provide an Alternate Employer endorsement in favor of Subcontractor. Where Subcontractor uses leased employees with Workers' Compensation insurance provided by a PEO or employee leasing company, Subcontractor is strictly prohibited from subletting any of its work without the express written agreement of Contractor.. Excess/Umbrella Liability (Occurrence Basis), $1,000,000 Per Accident = $2,000,000 Each Occurrence. Excess/Umbrella Liability (Occurrence Basis), Current ISO edition of CA 00 01 Arising out of any auto (Symbol 1), including owned, hired and nonowned = Such insurance shall be excess over and be no less broad than all coverages described above. Such insurance shall be endorsed to be primary and non-contributory to any liability insurance, whether primary, excess or umbrella, held by the Contractor Parties. rop-down coverage shall be provided for reduction and/or exhaustion of underlying aggregate limits and shall include a duty to defend any insured.
